---
title: "Interpreting Models"
subtitle: "Parameter estimates, confidence intervals and *p*-values"
author: "Professor Andy Field"
institute: "University of Sussex"
format: 
  revealjs:
    auto-play-media: true
    template-partials:
      - title-slide.html
    title-slide-attributes:
      data-background-image: ../shared_media/images/slide_background_title.jpg
      data-background-size: 100%
      data-background-position: center
      data-background-opacity: "1"
execute: 
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{r}
# general
library(easystats)
library(tidyverse)
# specific
library(metafor)


source("../helpers/discovr_helpers.R")
source("../helpers/easystats_helpers.R")
```


##  Learning outcomes 

::: incremental

Be able to interpret

- Parameter estimates
- Standardized parameter estimates
- Their confidence intervals
- *p*-values

:::

::: notes
Use C to toggle pen/markup
Use backspace to delete markup
Use f to toggle fullscreen
:::


## 

::: r-stack
![](../shared_media/images/spine_map.png){.fragment fig-align="center" width="1050" height="594"}

![](media/spine_map_lec_02.png){.fragment fig-align="center" width="1050" height="594"}
:::


##

![](../shared_media/images/dsr2_fig_04_39_workflow.png){fig-align="center" height=600}




# Interpreting parameter estimates

```{r, child = "parameters.qmd"}

```

# Standardized parameter estimates

```{r, child = "zparameters.qmd"}

```

# Confidence intervals

```{r, child = "ci.qmd"}

```


# Testing hypotheses

```{r, child = "nhst.qmd"}

```



## Summary

- To interpret models we need to look at a range of information
- The raw parameter estimate tells us the effect in units we measured
- The standardized parameter estimate tells us the effect in standard deviation units
  - Can be compared across studies that use different measures
- The confidence interval tells us about the uncertainty of our estimates
- The *p*-value tells us about statistical 'significance'
  - It must be interpretted within the context of the sample size and other information
